M03-title.title: Module 03
M03-title.subtitle: Soul System
M03-title.notes: The Soul layer answers "why it works." Opening session after lunch break — focus on re-energizing trainees.

M03-position.title: Positioning of the Soul System
M03-position.studentContent:
🦴 Bone
How to tell
Structure + Techniques
→
💜 Soul
Why it works
User Insights
→
🥩 Meat
How it looks & sounds
Next Module
Same skeleton, different soul = completely different results
M03-position.presenterContent:
📢 Presenter Script
This morning we learned the Bone layer — we now know "how to tell" using structures and techniques.

But with the same structure and techniques, why do some videos go viral while others fall flat?

That is what we will learn this afternoon — the "Soul layer" — it answers "why it works."
M03-position.notes: Establish the relationship between Bone and Soul. Emphasize that Soul is the analytical tool for "why it works."

M03-learning-goals.title: Learning Objectives
M03-learning-goals.studentContent:
After completing this module, you will be able to:

  • Understand the 5 fields of SoulProfile and their purposes
  • Analyze the "Soul layer" of any video using the 5 fields
  • Design a SoulProfile for a specific product
  • Understand how the Soul layer works together with the Bone layer
M03-learning-goals.notes: 4 learning objectives — the core focus is mastering SoulProfile.

M03-part1-title.title: Part 1
M03-part1-title.subtitle: SoulProfile
M03-part1-title.notes: Introduce the concept of SoulProfile and its 5 fields.

M03-1-what-is-soul.title: What Is SoulProfile?
M03-1-what-is-soul.studentContent:
SoulProfile = A video's "User Insight Profile"

It answers one core question:

"Why does this video resonate with people?"

SoulProfile contains 5 fields, each representing an "insight dimension"
M03-1-what-is-soul.presenterContent:
📢 Presenter Script
SoulProfile is our standardized description of a video's "Soul layer."

Think of it as a "User Insight Profile" — it documents why a particular video resonates with a specific audience.
M03-1-what-is-soul.notes: Establish the concept first, then expand on the 5 fields.

M03-1-five-fields.title: SoulProfile: The 5 Fields
M03-1-five-fields.studentContent:
Field 1

target_audience

Target Audience
"Who are we reaching?"

Field 2

trigger_scenario

Trigger Scenario
"When does it resonate?"

Field 3

reward_type

Reward Type
"What do they truly want?"

Field 4

conflict_type

Conflict Type
"Where is the tension?"

Field 5

emotion_arc

Emotion Arc
"How do emotions shift?"

💡 Memory Tip: Who (audience) → When (scenario) → What they want (reward) → What tension (conflict) → Emotion shift (emotion)
M03-1-five-fields.notes: Overview of the 5 fields — each will be explained in detail later.

M03-part2-title.title: Part 2
M03-part2-title.subtitle: target_audience — Target Audience
M03-part2-title.notes: The first and most important field.

M03-2-definition.title: target_audience Definition
M03-2-definition.studentContent:
target_audience
Who is the "ideal viewer" this video aims to persuade?
Not "who the product is for," but "who this video will move"
Dimensions included:
age_rangeAge range (e.g., 25-35)
genderGender tendency
pain_pointsCore pain point list
consumption_levelSpending power
M03-2-definition.presenterContent:
📢 Presenter Script
The first field is target_audience — the target audience. It seems basic, but most people get it wrong.

I often hear: "Our product is for everyone." That is a product perspective, not a content perspective.

A product may suit many people, but a single video can only move one type of person. This is the first critical insight of the Soul layer.
M03-2-definition.notes: Emphasize the core idea: "a single video can only move one type of person."

M03-2-misconception.title: Common Misconception: The Broader the Better?
M03-2-misconception.studentContent:
❌
Wrong Thinking
"The broader the audience, the more views"
✅
Right Understanding
"The more precise the audience, the deeper the impact"
A single video can only move one type of person
Trying to move everyone = moving no one
M03-2-misconception.notes: Correct the common misconception of "the broader the better."

M03-2-example.title: Case Study: Same Product, Different Audiences
M03-2-example.studentContent:
Take skincare as an example — the same product can have completely different videos:
Target Audience Video Angle Core Appeal HOOK Example
25yo Night Owls Emergency rescue, fast results "Meeting a client tomorrow" "After-hours skin rescue! 10-minute fix for dull skin"
35yo Moms with Early Aging Daily care, long-term maintenance "Love yourself even when busy" "A busy mom-of-three's skincare secret..."
20yo College Students Affordable alternatives, value for money "Students can use quality products too" "Luxury cream results on a student budget"
💡 Insight: Same product, three completely different videos, reaching three entirely different audiences. That is the power of target_audience.
M03-2-example.notes: Use a concrete case to show the differentiation value of target_audience.

M03-part3-title.title: Part 3
M03-part3-title.subtitle: trigger_scenario — Trigger Scenario
M03-part3-title.notes: The second field — about "when people are triggered."

M03-3-definition.title: trigger_scenario Definition
M03-3-definition.studentContent:
trigger_scenario
Under what circumstances will the viewer be moved by this content?
Great content can "summon" specific scenario memories in the viewer
Scenario Types:
TimeSeasonal change, after staying up late, early morning, before period
PlaceIn the office, on the subway, at the vanity
EventSeeing someone with great skin, being told "you look tired"
EmotionFeeling anxious, bored, wanting to treat yourself
M03-3-definition.presenterContent:
📢 Presenter Script
The second field is trigger_scenario — the trigger scenario.

Have you ever had this experience: you see the same ad all the time and think nothing of it, but at one particular moment, it suddenly hits you?

That is the power of scenario. Great content can "summon" specific scenario memories in the viewer.
M03-3-definition.notes: Scenario triggering is the key mechanism for content that "touches hearts."

M03-3-types.title: Scenario Types in Detail
M03-3-types.studentContent:
⏰ Time Scenario
"My skin always acts up when the seasons change"
"Waking up every day with panda eyes"
"Always breaking out before my period"
📍 Place Scenario
"My face feels tight and dry in the office AC"
"My skin breaks out whenever I travel for work"
"Seeing my pores in the gym mirror"
📌 Event Scenario
"A coworker asked 'Did you not sleep well?'"
"My friend's skin suddenly got so much better"
"Seeing my own face on a video call"
💭 Emotion Scenario
"Feeling so stressed lately, want to treat myself"
"Work is exhausting, want a little pick-me-up"
"Had a fight with my partner, feeling down"
M03-3-types.notes: 4 scenario types: Time, Place, Event, Emotion.

M03-3-practice.title: Activity: Scenario Brainstorm
M03-3-practice.studentContent:
🤝 Interactive Activity - 5 Minutes
Task: For your product, list as many trigger scenarios as possible

Rules:

  • 2-minute brainstorm per group
  • Write one scenario per sticky note
  • Think across all four dimensions: Time, Place, Event, Emotion
  • The more specific the scenario, the better
💡 Tip: A good scenario description makes people instantly relate — "Yes, yes, that is exactly me!"
M03-3-practice.presenterContent:
▶ Presenter Action:
  • Distribute sticky notes to each group
  • Set a 2-minute timer
  • Collect sticky notes and post them on the whiteboard
  • Quickly highlight a few good scenarios
M03-3-practice.notes: Interactive activity to re-energize trainees after lunch break.

M03-3-case1.title: Case 1: Morning-Night Serum "Time x Emotion"
M03-3-case1.studentContent:
🎬 Scenario Reconstruction
"7 AM, the alarm goes off. You drag yourself out of bed and stumble to the bathroom.

After washing your face, you lean in close to the mirror out of habit — stayed up binge-watching last night, your complexion is dull, and those smile lines seem even deeper...

You touch your face — skin rough as sandpaper.

'I'm only 25, why do I look 35? Starting today, I HAVE to take skincare seriously...'"
📝 Scenario Element Breakdown
⏰ Time
After waking up in the morning
📍 Place
In front of the bathroom mirror
🎯 Event
Looking in the mirror after staying up late
💭 Emotion
Age anxiety + determination
🔍 Why Does This Scenario Work?
1. Morning = the moment skin issues are most visible
Just-woken skin is at its most raw — no makeup to cover anything, and late-night damage is fully exposed. This is when users are most prone to skincare anxiety.
2. "Leaning in close to the mirror" = universal behavioral resonance
Nearly every woman has the habit of leaning close to the mirror to examine her skin. The moment you describe this action, people instantly visualize it — immersion is immediate.
3. Age comparison = core anxiety trigger
"25 but looking 35" directly hits the core pain point of early-aging serum users — fighting premature aging. This age-gap framing is far more impactful than simply saying "bad skin."
💡 Application: Best posted on Monday mornings, aligning with the "start the new week with proper skincare" mindset
M03-3-case1.presenterContent:
▶ Presenter Action:
  • Ask trainees: "Have you ever woken up, looked in the mirror, and been shocked by your skin?"
  • Emphasize: Morning is the golden posting time for skincare content
  • Point out: "Age anxiety" is the core trigger for anti-aging products
M03-3-case1.notes: Case 1: Morning-Night Serum — demonstrates the power of combining Time x Emotion.

M03-3-case2.title: Case 2: Sunscreen "Event x Emotion"
M03-3-case2.studentContent:
🎬 Scenario Reconstruction
"Went to the beach with friends over the weekend — had a blast.

Back home after a shower, looking in the mirror — a clear watch-strap tan line on my arm, face two shades lighter than my neck...

Posted a selfie to the group chat, friend replies: 'Girl, did you not wear sunscreen?'

'Oh no... how long is it going to take to get my color back...'"
📝 Scenario Element Breakdown
⏰ Time
After outdoor activities
📍 Place
At home after a shower
🎯 Event
Discovering sunburn/tan
💭 Emotion
Regret + anxiety
🔍 Why Does This Scenario Work?
1. "Watch-strap tan line" = a visualized pain point
Getting tanned is not abstract — specific details like "watch-strap marks" and "color difference" make users instantly recall their own similar experiences, creating powerful immersion.
2. Friend's comment = social pressure trigger
Other people's remarks often hit harder than self-examination. A friend saying "Didn't you wear sunscreen?" creates more anxiety than discovering the tan yourself.
3. Regret psychology = high conversion intent
The feeling of "I should have worn sunscreen" converts directly into strong purchase intent — users want to buy immediately to avoid repeating the mistake.
💡 Application: Post after holidays like May Day or National Day, paired with "post-holiday tan rescue" topics
M03-3-case2.presenterContent:
▶ Presenter Action:
  • Ask trainees: "Have you ever come back from a trip and found yourself tanned?"
  • Emphasize: Sunscreen products need scenarios tied to the "regret" emotion
  • Point out: Before and after holidays is the golden period for sunscreen content
M03-3-case2.notes: Case 2: Sunscreen — demonstrates the power of combining Event x Emotion.

M03-3-case3.title: Case 3: Concealer "Place x Event"
M03-3-case3.studentContent:
🎬 Scenario Reconstruction
"Office restroom, touching up makeup after lunch break.

Pull out your mirror — after 3 meetings this morning, foundation is patchy, dark circles showing through again...

There is an important client pitch this afternoon — how can I show up looking like this?

'I should have bought a better concealer. The one I have barely covers anything...'"
📝 Scenario Element Breakdown
⏰ Time
Lunch break touch-up
📍 Place
Office restroom
🎯 Event
Discovering makeup has worn off / dark circles showing
💭 Emotion
Embarrassment + urgency
🔍 Why Does This Scenario Work?
1. Office restroom = a high-frequency scenario for working women
This space is instantly recognizable — mention "touching up in the office restroom" and every working woman can picture it immediately. It is the golden scenario for cosmetics brands.
2. Important meeting = appearance pressure amplified
A "client pitch" is not just any occasion — this detail amplifies the user's makeup anxiety. The more important the occasion, the more urgent the product need becomes.
3. Current product not working = motivation to switch
"The one I have barely covers anything" is the core trigger for brand-switching. The user already owns a concealer but it is not good enough — that is exactly your product's opportunity.
💡 Application: Post between 12-2 PM on weekdays to precisely reach the touch-up scenario
M03-3-case3.presenterContent:
▶ Presenter Action:
  • Ask trainees: "Is the lunch-break touch-up a high-frequency scenario for working women?"
  • Emphasize: Cosmetics products are especially suited for "emergency" and "rescue" scenarios
  • Point out: Scenarios should include the implicit message that "current solutions are not good enough"
M03-3-case3.notes: Case 3: Concealer — demonstrates the power of combining Place x Event.

M03-3-case4.title: Case 4: Eye Cream/Eye Mask "Time x Place"
M03-3-case4.studentContent:
🎬 Scenario Reconstruction
"11 PM, finally done scrolling through that last short video, put the phone down.

Head to the bathroom to wash up, flip on the light — the reflection in the mirror startles you —

Eyes dry and irritated, fine lines around the eyes clearly visible, eye bags puffy...

'Staying up late scrolling every night... my eyes are going to be ruined. I HAVE to buy an eye mask tomorrow...'"
📝 Scenario Element Breakdown
⏰ Time
Late at night before bed
📍 Place
Under bright bathroom lights
🎯 Event
Discovering eye area issues after staying up late
💭 Emotion
Shock + self-blame
🔍 Why Does This Scenario Work?
1. Late night 11 PM = peak guilt from staying up late
Late at night is a time for self-reflection, and the guilt from staying up late makes users more likely to make "remedial" decisions. Eye care products perfectly align with this emotional state.
2. "Flipping on the light" = dramatic impact
Switching from the dim glow of a phone screen to the harsh bathroom light exposes skin issues instantly. This "lights-on moment" is a classic trigger scenario for eye care products.
3. Scrolling on the phone = a universal modern habit
Nearly everyone scrolls on their phone before bed. Tying late nights to phone scrolling makes users think: "That is exactly what I do!"
💡 Application: Post between 9-11 PM to precisely reach the "night owl" audience
M03-3-case4.presenterContent:
▶ Presenter Action:
  • Ask trainees: "Does your brand have eye care products? Would this scenario fit?"
  • Emphasize: The core scenario for eye products is "staying up late + self-blame"
  • Point out: Evening is the golden posting window for eye care content
M03-3-case4.notes: Case 4: Eye Cream/Eye Mask — demonstrates the power of combining Time x Place.

M03-3-case5.title: Case 5: Acne Treatment "Four-Dimension Combo"
M03-3-case5.studentContent:
🎬 Scenario Reconstruction
"The night before a date, washing your face and looking in the mirror.

A pimple has popped up on your forehead — red and impossible to miss. You can't help but squeeze it...

The more you squeeze, the redder it gets. The redder it gets, the more you panic. How am I going to face them tomorrow?

Tossing and turning, unable to sleep, you open Xiaohongshu and search 'emergency acne fix before a date'..."
📝 Scenario Element Breakdown All Four Dimensions
⏰ Time
The night before a date/important event
📍 Place
In front of the bathroom mirror
🎯 Event
Discovering a pimple + can't resist squeezing
💭 Emotion
Anxiety + regret + insomnia
🔍 Why Is This a "Perfect Score" Scenario?
1. Night before a date = peak appearance anxiety
Before an important social occasion, appearance sensitivity is multiplied by 10. Tiny imperfections that normally go unnoticed become magnified.
2. Squeezing a pimple = universal behavior + regret cycle
Knowing you shouldn't squeeze it but doing it anyway, then regretting it — this psychological cycle is so real that nearly everyone has been through it.
3. Insomnia + searching = high conversion intent
So anxious they cannot sleep, actively searching for solutions — these users are already at the bottom of the "purchase funnel" with extremely high conversion rates.
⭐ Perfect Score Formula: All four dimensions are specific, emotionally charged, and relatable = the user is instantly immersed
M03-3-case5.presenterContent:
▶ Presenter Action:
  • Ask trainees: "How many dimensions does this scenario use?" (Answer: all four)
  • Emphasize: "Emergency" scenarios have the highest conversion for acne products
  • Summarize: Post "date-night emergency" content before weekends/holidays
M03-3-case5.notes: Case 5: Acne Treatment — demonstrates the power of combining all four dimensions.

M03-3-summary.title: Scenario Design: Pattern Summary of 5 Cases
M03-3-summary.studentContent:
Case Primary Dimensions Core Emotion Application
Morning-Night Serum Time x Emotion Age anxiety + determination Post on Monday mornings
Sunscreen Event x Emotion Regret + anxiety After holidays / before trips
Concealer Place x Event Embarrassment + urgency Post at noon on workdays
Eye Cream/Eye Mask Time x Place Shock + self-blame Post between 9-11 PM
Acne Treatment All Four Dimensions Anxiety + regret Before weekends/holidays
📐 Scenario Design Formula
Specific Time + Familiar Place + Trigger Event + Strong Emotion
= A scenario users can immerse in within 3 seconds
✅ Validation Criteria
1. The user responds with "Yes, that is exactly me!"
2. The scenario includes action details (squeezing pimples, leaning into the mirror, touching up)
3. There is an inner monologue (that one key thought)
4. It sparks emotional resonance (not describing, but evoking)
M03-3-summary.presenterContent:
▶ Presenter Action:
  • Quickly review the key takeaways from all 5 cases
  • Emphasize: You do not need all four dimensions, but be specific enough
  • Give trainees 30 seconds to jot down the formula
M03-3-summary.notes: Pattern summary of scenario design — help trainees form a methodology.

M03-part4-title.title: Part 4
M03-part4-title.subtitle: reward_type — Reward Type
M03-part4-title.notes: The third field — about "what they truly want deep down."

M03-4-definition.title: reward_type Definition
M03-4-definition.studentContent:
reward_type
What does the viewer truly want deep down?
Psychological needs that go beyond the product itself
People do not buy skincare for the sake of skincare
They buy it for "beauty," "confidence," "validation"
M03-4-definition.presenterContent:
📢 Presenter Script
The third field is reward_type — psychological motivation.

Note that we are talking about needs that go "beyond the product itself." People do not buy skincare for the sake of skincare — they buy it for "beauty," "confidence," or "validation."

Understanding this is crucial, because content that stays at the product level feels like a "hard sell." Content that reaches the psychological motivation level creates genuine resonance.
M03-4-definition.notes: Emphasize the "beyond the product" deep-level needs.

M03-4-types.title: Common Reward Types
M03-4-types.studentContent:
Motivation Description Viewer's Inner Voice Content Expression Example
beauty Look better "I want to be attractive" "Colleagues asked what skincare I switched to"
money Save/earn money "I want a good deal" "Get the same results others pay XX for"
time Save time "I want efficiency" "Full look in 3 minutes flat"
safety Security "I want peace of mind" "Safe formula even for pregnant women"
confidence Confidence "I want to be recognized" "Finally confident enough to go bare-faced"
control Sense of control "I want to be in charge" "My skin, my rules"
health Health "I want to be healthy" "Healthy skin matters more than makeup"

M03-4-mapping.title: Motivation x Content Angle
M03-4-mapping.studentContent:
Same product — different psychological motivations dictate different content angles:
Motivation: beauty
Content angle: Emphasize results and transformation
HOOK: "After two weeks, my skin is literally glowing"
Motivation: money
Content angle: Emphasize value for money
HOOK: "Luxury dupe! Same results at 1/5 the price"
Motivation: confidence
Content angle: Emphasize social validation
HOOK: "The feeling of being asked 'How did your skin get so good'"
💡 Key Insight: Find the right psychological motivation and your content gains a "soul." Product-level content is a "hard sell" — motivation-level content is genuine resonance.
M03-4-mapping.notes: Show how motivation influences the content angle.

M03-part5-title.title: Part 5
M03-part5-title.subtitle: conflict_type — Conflict Type
M03-part5-title.notes: The fourth field — this is the key to creating "tension."

M03-5-definition.title: conflict_type Definition
M03-5-definition.studentContent:
conflict_type
What is the "tension" or "contradiction" the video needs to resolve?
No conflict means no story; no story means no appeal
Great content always has "tension"
Tension = Appeal
M03-5-definition.presenterContent:
📢 Presenter Script
The fourth field is conflict_type — core conflict. This field is extremely important.

Why? Because no conflict means no story, and no story means no appeal.

Great content always has "tension." This tension can come from many sources: inner anxiety, external judgment, before-and-after comparisons...

M03-5-types.title: 5 Conflict Types
M03-5-types.studentContent:
Type Description Examples
internal
Internal Conflict		 Self vs. own anxiety "I know staying up late is bad, but I can't stop"
"Want to look better but too lazy to do skincare"
external
External Conflict		 Self vs. outside judgment "People say my skin looks bad"
"Being asked if I didn't sleep well"
comparison
Comparison Conflict		 Before vs. After "Before using vs. after using"
"Their skin vs. my skin"
cost
Cost Conflict		 Want vs. Price to pay "Want results but think it's expensive"
"Want good skin but no time"
misconception
Misconception Conflict		 Wrong belief vs. Truth "What you think vs. the reality"
"90% of people get this wrong"

M03-5-guide.title: Conflict Type Selection Guide
M03-5-guide.studentContent:
Different product types suit different conflicts:
Skincare / Cosmetics
Recommended: internal (inner anxiety) + external (outside judgment)
"Know I should do skincare but keep putting it off" + "Being told my skin looks bad"
Tool Products
Recommended: cost (cost conflict) + misconception (misconception)
"Want efficiency but tools seem expensive" + "You think it's hard but it's actually easy"
Food / Health Supplements
Recommended: internal (health anxiety) + comparison (before-after comparison)
"Know I should eat healthy but can't" + "Changes after one month"
High-Ticket Products
Recommended: cost (price concern) + misconception (value perception)
"Think it's expensive" + "Actually cheaper per use than XX"
M03-5-guide.notes: Help trainees choose the right conflict type for their products.

M03-6-definition.title: emotion_arc Definition
M03-6-definition.studentContent:
emotion_arc
How do the viewer's emotions shift throughout the video?
Emotional shifts are designed, not accidental
That feeling of being "carried along" while watching a video
From curiosity to empathy, then anticipation, then wanting to buy
This is not accidental — it is by design
M03-6-definition.presenterContent:
📢 Presenter Script
The final field is emotion_arc — the emotion arc.

Have you ever watched a video where your emotions were carried along — from curiosity to empathy, then anticipation, then wanting to buy immediately?

That is not accidental — it is designed. Every section has its own emotional task.
M03-6-definition.notes: Emphasize that emotions are "designed," not accidental.

M03-6-structure.title: Section x Emotion Mapping
M03-6-structure.studentContent:
Section Typical Emotion Emotional Task
HOOK Curiosity / Surprise / Anxiety The emotional starting point that grabs attention — "Wait, what is this?"
BRIDGE Empathy / Agreement Building connection — "You get me"
PROBLEM Unease / Anxiety Creating tension — "What do I do?"
RESOLUTION Hope / Anticipation Releasing tension — "There is a solution!"
VALUE Trust / Reassurance Stabilizing the decision — "Worth buying"
CTA Urgency / Action desire Driving action — "I need it now!"
💡 Typical Emotion Arc: Curiosity → Empathy → Unease → Hope → Trust → Action
M03-6-structure.notes: Show the mapping between Section and emotion.

M03-part7-title.title: Part 7
M03-part7-title.subtitle: Comprehensive Case Analysis
M03-part7-title.notes: Use complete cases to connect all 5 fields.

M03-7-template.title: SoulProfile Complete Template
M03-7-template.studentContent:
Designing the Soul of a viral video requires filling in 5 fields:
SoulProfile Field Core Question
target_audience Who to move? (Specific persona, not "all women")
trigger_scenario What scenario triggers it? (Time + Place + Event + Emotion)
reward_type What do they truly want? (Psychological need beyond product features)
conflict_type What is the core conflict? (Internal / External / Comparison / Cost / Misconception)
emotion_arc How does the emotion arc flow? (Emotional shift from beginning → middle → end)
💡 Next up: 5 beauty/skincare cases showing how to design a complete SoulProfile
M03-7-template.presenterContent: ▶ Presenter Action: Quickly walk through the template and tell trainees that the next 5 cases will explain it in detail.
M03-7-template.notes: Provide the complete template to set the stage for the cases.

M03-7-case1.title: Case 1: Anti-Aging Serum | Complete SoulProfile
M03-7-case1.studentContent:
target_audience
28-35yo working women
Mid-to-high income, starting to care about anti-aging but without an established routine yet, anxious about "early aging" but not ready to admit it
trigger_scenario
Waking up and noticing smile lines in the mirror
Time: Monday morning | Place: Bathroom mirror | Event: Skincare after a late night | Emotion: Age anxiety
reward_type
CONTROL — Sense of control
"I can slow down aging and control how old I look" — not about beauty, but about mastering time
conflict_type
Internal conflict: Wanting to stay young vs. not wanting to admit aging
Users avoid saying "anti-aging," preferring "maintenance" — this psychology must be respected
emotion_arc
Anxiety😰 → Empathy😢 → Hope✨ → Action💪
HOOK triggers anxiety → PROBLEM resonates with pain → VALUE offers solution → CTA encourages action
📹 Video Content Preview
HOOK 0-3s
"25 years old with the smile lines of a 35-year-old?"
→ Emotion: 😰 Anxiety
PROBLEM 3-15s
"Every day I look in the mirror and feel a little older. Tried so many luxury creams, nothing works..."
→ Emotion: 😢 Empathy
VALUE 15-50s
"Then I discovered the key to anti-aging is not price — it is ingredient formulation..." (show product)
→ Emotion: ✨ Hope
CTA 50-60s
"Start anti-aging early — tap the cart, this price won't last"
→ Emotion: 💪 Action
M03-7-case1.presenterContent:
▶ Presenter Action:
  • Walk through each of the 5 fields and how they work together
  • Emphasize: emotion_arc is a design that runs throughout
  • Ask trainees: "How could you apply this to your brand's anti-aging products?"
M03-7-case1.notes: Case 1: Anti-Aging Serum — complete SoulProfile demonstration.

M03-7-case2.title: Case 2: Brightening Serum | Complete SoulProfile
M03-7-case2.studentContent:
target_audience
22-30yo students / early-career women
Dull or uneven skin tone, want brighter skin but on a budget, easily attracted by "fast results"
trigger_scenario
Noticing skin tone difference in a group photo with friends
Time: After a gathering | Place: Looking at phone photos | Event: Realizing you are the darkest one | Emotion: Insecurity + envy
reward_type
confidence — Confidence
"No longer being the darkest one in friend group photos" — not just about brightness, but social confidence
conflict_type
Comparison conflict: Others are fair vs. I am not
Use comparison to amplify the pain: "After my roommate used it for a month, our skin tones match now"
emotion_arc
Insecurity😔 → Envy😍 → Anticipation🌟 → Confidence😊
From "I look so dull" → "How did she get so bright" → "I can do it too" → "Buy it!"
📹 Video Content Preview
HOOK 0-3s
"In every group photo, I always hide at the edge, because..." (show comparison photo)
→ Emotion: 😔 Insecurity
BRIDGE 3-10s
"Until my roommate asked: 'What have you been using?' I found out she was secretly using this..."
→ Emotion: 😍 Envy + curiosity
VALUE 10-45s
"After one month, I finally don't have to hide at the edge of group photos" (show before/after)
→ Emotion: 🌟 Anticipation
CTA 45-60s
"Want the same glow-up? Link is right here — get bright before summer!"
→ Emotion: 😊 Confidence
M03-7-case2.presenterContent:
▶ Presenter Action:
  • Emphasize: The reward for brightening products is not "brightness" — it is "social confidence"
  • Point out: Comparison conflict is the most effective conflict type for brightening products
  • Ask trainees: "How would you design the comparison for your brightening products?"
M03-7-case2.notes: Case 2: Brightening Serum — complete SoulProfile demonstration.

M03-7-case3.title: Case 3: Acne Treatment | Complete SoulProfile
M03-7-case3.studentContent:
target_audience
18-28yo people with recurring acne
Have tried many acne products with no lasting results, somewhat hopeless but unwilling to give up, extremely sensitive about "bad skin"
trigger_scenario
Sudden breakout before an important event
Time: Night before a date/interview | Place: In front of the mirror | Event: Finding new pimples/squeezing | Emotion: Anxiety + despair
reward_type
confidence — Confidence
"No more hiding, confidently showing my face" — the confidence to face the world without worry
conflict_type
Misconception conflict: Thought it was a skin problem, actually a method problem
"I used to think my skin was just bad, then I realized I was using the wrong approach"
emotion_arc
Despair😭 → Empathy🥺 → Epiphany💡 → Renewed hope🔥
From "Am I a lost cause" → "It's not my fault" → "Found the answer" → "This time it will work"
📹 Video Content Preview
HOOK 0-3s
"5 years of bad skin, tried over 20 acne products — I thought this was my life..."
→ Emotion: 😭 Despair
PROBLEM 3-15s
"Every time it starts to clear up, season changes and it flares up again. The cycle is absolutely crushing..."
→ Emotion: 🥺 Empathy
REVERSAL 15-25s
"Then I learned that recurring acne is not because your skin is bad — it is because your skin barrier was never repaired..."
→ Emotion: 💡 Epiphany
VALUE 25-50s
"This one tackles both acne AND barrier repair..." (show product and results)
→ Emotion: 🌟 Hope
CTA 50-60s
"You are not alone in the acne struggle — link is right here for you"
→ Emotion: 🔥 Action
M03-7-case3.presenterContent:
▶ Presenter Action:
  • Emphasize: Acne product users are already quite hopeless — give them an emotional outlet first
  • Point out: "Misconception conflict" can relieve the user's self-blame
  • Ask trainees: "What differentiates your acne products? How would you design the REVERSAL?"
M03-7-case3.notes: Case 3: Acne Treatment — complete SoulProfile demonstration.

M03-7-case4.title: Case 4: Lipstick/Lip Glaze | Complete SoulProfile
M03-7-case4.studentContent:
target_audience
20-32yo working/dating women
Want "polished but effortless," want healthy color without heavy makeup look, responsive to "brightens skin tone" and "adds elegance"
trigger_scenario
Quick touch-up before heading out
Time: 5 minutes before leaving | Place: Entryway/office restroom | Event: Looking in the mirror and seeing a washed-out complexion | Emotion: Rushed + insecure
reward_type
beauty — Beauty
"Put on lipstick and everything changes" — the desire to look more attractive instantly
conflict_type
Comparison conflict: Dramatic before vs. after contrast
Bare-faced and washed out vs. instant color boost — visual impact is the core of lipstick content
emotion_arc
Neutral😐 → Amazed😍 → Captivated💓 → Must-buy🛒
The core of lipstick content is the "wow moment" — emotions need to ramp up fast
📹 Video Content Preview
HOOK 0-3s
(Bare face) "Watch me transform in 3 seconds!"
→ Emotion: 😐 Neutral/curious
BRIDGE 3-8s
(Close-up of applying lipstick) "Just this one stick can rescue any look..."
→ Emotion: 🤔 Anticipation
VALUE 8-40s
(Show results) "Brightens any skin tone, flattering on everyone, no transfer..." (various scene demos)
→ Emotion: 😍 Amazed
CTA 40-50s
"This shade is absolutely incredible — selling out fast, go go go!"
→ Emotion: 🛒 Must-buy
💄 Lipstick Content Characteristics: Emotion arc is short and sharp — the "wow moment" of visual impact is everything
M03-7-case4.presenterContent:
▶ Presenter Action:
  • Emphasize: Cosmetics content emotion arcs need to be "short and sharp"
  • Point out: The core of lipstick content is "contrast impact" — the bigger the before/after gap, the better
  • Ask trainees: "How would you design the wow moment for your cosmetics products?"
M03-7-case4.notes: Case 4: Lipstick/Lip Glaze — complete SoulProfile demonstration.

M03-7-case5.title: Case 5: Sheet Mask | Complete SoulProfile
M03-7-case5.studentContent:
target_audience
25-40yo busy women
High work-life stress, no time for elaborate skincare, dry and dull skin, want "effortless skincare"
trigger_scenario
Late-night skincare after working overtime
Time: After 10 PM | Place: Bedroom/sofa | Event: Finally able to lie down | Emotion: Exhausted + wanting to treat yourself
reward_type
health — Health
"Lying down with a mask on is the most relaxing moment of the day" — caring for skin health as a form of self-care
conflict_type
Cost conflict: Wanting proper skincare vs. no time or energy
"I know I should take care of my skin, but I'm just too tired" — sheet masks solve this dilemma
emotion_arc
Exhausted😫 → Empathy🥹 → Relaxed😌 → Satisfied😊
Sheet mask content emotions go "from tense to relaxed" — create a soothing atmosphere
📹 Video Content Preview
HOOK 0-3s
"Worked until 10 PM, skin so dry it's flaking, but I'm too exhausted to move..."
→ Emotion: 😫 Exhausted
PROBLEM 3-12s
"Want to do proper skincare, but cleanser-toner-serum-moisturizer-cream — just thinking about it is exhausting..."
→ Emotion: 🥹 Empathy
VALUE 12-45s
"So now I just use this — slap it on, lie down for 20 minutes, scroll my phone, works better than a full routine..." (show usage + results)
→ Emotion: 😌 Relaxed
CTA 45-55s
"After a long day, you deserve these 20 minutes of relaxation — link is right here"
→ Emotion: 😊 Satisfied
🧖 Sheet Mask Content Characteristics: Emotion arc should be "therapeutic" — from tense to relaxed, creating a ritual feeling
M03-7-case5.presenterContent:
▶ Presenter Action:
  • Emphasize: The reward of sheet masks is not just skincare results — it is the ritual of "treating yourself"
  • Point out: Cost conflict works for all products positioned as "time-saving and effortless"
  • Summarize: 5 cases showcase 5 different SoulProfile design approaches
M03-7-case5.notes: Case 5: Sheet Mask — complete SoulProfile demonstration.

M03-7-comparison.title: 5-Case Comparison: Soul Design Across Products
M03-7-comparison.studentContent:
Product target_audience reward_type conflict_type emotion_arc
Anti-Aging Serum 28-35yo working women CONTROL Internal conflict Anxiety→Empathy→Hope→Action
Brightening Serum 22-30yo students/early career confidence Comparison conflict Insecurity→Envy→Anticipation→Confidence
Acne Treatment 18-28yo recurring acne confidence Misconception conflict Despair→Empathy→Epiphany→Hope
Lipstick/Lip Glaze 20-32yo working/dating beauty Comparison conflict Neutral→Amazed→Captivated→Must-buy
Sheet Mask 25-40yo busy women health Cost conflict Exhausted→Empathy→Relaxed→Satisfied
💡 Key Findings
• Even within skincare, reward_type is completely different
• Different conflict types create different emotion arcs
• emotion_arc must align with video structure (Bone)
✅ Design Sequence
1️⃣ First define target_audience (who to move)
2️⃣ Then define trigger_scenario (what scenario)
3️⃣ Then reward + conflict (motivation + tension)
4️⃣ Finally design emotion_arc (emotional flow)
M03-7-comparison.presenterContent:
▶ Presenter Action:
  • Quickly compare the differences across all 5 cases
  • Emphasize: Even within the same category, Soul design can be completely different
  • Give trainees 30 seconds to think about the Soul design for their own products

M03-7-integration.title: Section x Strategy x Soul: The Trinity
M03-7-integration.studentContent:
The relationship between these three is not side-by-side — it is nested layers
🎯
Soul
Core - Who to move
→
Section
Structure - What comes first/last
→
Strategy
Technique - How to express
→
Script
Actual content
Actual Script = Soul (all 5 fields) x Section position x Strategy technique
Soul is not split and mapped to individual Sections — it works as a whole to shape the content at each position
💡 Example: Same HOOK + PAIN strategy, but different Souls produce completely different scripts
Soul A: 28-35yo working women + age anxiety + control
"25 years old with the smile lines of a 35-year-old?"
→ Pain comes from: target_audience's age anxiety + conflict_type's internal struggle
Soul B: 18-25yo students + social insecurity + confidence
"In every group photo, I always hide at the edge..."
→ Pain comes from: target_audience's social scenario + reward_type's desire for confidence
⚡ Same Section (HOOK) + Same Strategy (PAIN), but different Soul → completely different scripts
M03-7-integration.presenterContent:
▶ Presenter Action:
  • Key emphasis: Soul works as a whole — it is not split and mapped one-to-one
  • Use the two examples to compare: same structure + strategy, different Soul → completely different content
  • Analogy: Soul is the "spirit," Section is the "skeleton," Strategy is the "moves," Script is the "final performance"
M03-7-integration.notes: Section x Strategy x Soul — the correct understanding of the trinity.

M03-7-fullcase.title: Live Demo: Acne Serum Complete Creative Process
M03-7-fullcase.studentContent:
🎯 Soul Core (Holistic)
TA: 18-25yo students with recurring acne
Scenario: Breakout the night before a date
Motivation: confidence
Conflict: Misconception (method, not skin)
Arc: Despair→Empathy→Epiphany→Hope→Action
📐 Skeleton: HPRVRC
HOOK → PROBLEM → REVERSAL → VALUE → CTA
💡 Key Understanding
The Soul on the left is one whole
It influences every single line of script on the right
📝 Soul (holistic) x Section x Strategy → Actual Script
HOOK x PAIN emotion_arc: 😭Despair
"5 years of bad skin — I thought this was my life..."
← Soul influence: TA's age + scenario anxiety + conflict's sense of despair together shape this line
PROBLEM x PAIN+STORY emotion_arc: 🥺Empathy
"Tried over 20 acne products — clears up then comes right back..."
← Soul influence: TA's repeated battle with acne + misconception conflict's "wrong path" together shape this
REVERSAL x EXPLAIN emotion_arc: 💡Epiphany
"Then I learned: recurring acne is not bad skin — it is an unrepaired barrier!"
← Soul influence: Misconception conflict type directly determines the reversal content as "correcting a belief"
VALUE x DEMO+PROOF emotion_arc: ✨Hope
"This one tackles acne AND repairs the barrier..." (show results)
← Soul influence: confidence motivation means emphasizing "confidence to show your face"
CTA x URGENCY emotion_arc: 🔥Action
"You are not alone in this fight — limited stock!"
← Soul influence: TA's sense of isolation + confidence motivation → "you are not alone"
M03-7-fullcase.presenterContent:
▶ Presenter Action:
  • Focus on explaining the "Soul influence" note under each line of script
  • Emphasize: One Soul influences ALL Sections — it is not a one-to-one mapping
  • Ask trainees: "If we change the conflict_type to 'comparison conflict,' how would REVERSAL change?"
M03-7-fullcase.notes: Complete creative process demo — emphasize how the holistic Soul shapes each Section's actual content.

M03-7-fullcase2.title: Live Case 2: Sunscreen | External Conflict
M03-7-fullcase2.studentContent:
🎯 Soul Core (Holistic)
TA: 20-30yo women who love going out but fear tanning
Scenario: Discovering a tan after a beach/outdoor trip
Motivation: safety (protect skin)
Conflict: External (UV rays vs. wanting to go out)
Arc: Regret→Fear→Reassurance→Action
📐 Skeleton: HBVC
HOOK → BRIDGE → VALUE → CTA
HOOK x FEAR emotion: 😱Regret
"One trip out and I came back two shades darker..."
← Soul influence: outdoor scenario + consequences of external conflict + TA's fear of tanning
BRIDGE x STORY emotion: 😰Fear→Empathy
"I used to think sunscreen was too much hassle too, until that trip to the beach..."
← Soul influence: TA's complacency + real scenario experience builds trust
VALUE x DEMO+PROOF emotion: 😌Reassurance
"SPF50+, fast-absorbing, no white cast — all-day outdoor protection without worry"
← Soul influence: safety motivation means emphasizing "protection" + countering external threat
CTA x URGENCY emotion: 💪Action
"Stock up before your next trip — link is here, don't regret it after!"
← Soul influence: scenario timing (pre-holiday) + evoking "regret" fear
M03-7-fullcase2.presenterContent: ▶ Presenter Key Point: The core of external conflict is "the enemy is out there" (UV rays), so HOOK uses FEAR to create urgency, and CTA uses "don't regret it" to reinforce.
M03-7-fullcase2.notes: Sunscreen case — demonstrates the creative logic of external conflict type.

M03-7-fullcase3.title: Live Case 3: Eye Cream | Cost Conflict
M03-7-fullcase3.studentContent:
🎯 Soul Core (Holistic)
TA: 25-35yo late-night workers/binge-watchers
Scenario: Noticing eye wrinkles in the mirror after scrolling
Motivation: CONTROL (fighting late-night damage)
Conflict: Cost conflict (want to stay up vs. fear aging)
Arc: Shock→Self-blame→Relief→Action
📐 Skeleton: HPRVC
HOOK → PROBLEM → RESOLUTION → VALUE → CTA
HOOK x PAIN emotion: 😨Shock
"Staying up feels great in the moment, but the eye wrinkles keep getting longer..."
← Soul influence: Core of cost conflict — the "price" of staying up late is eye area aging
PROBLEM x STORY emotion: 😔Self-blame
"I know I should sleep early, but I just can't put the phone down..."
← Soul influence: TA's real state + cost conflict's "knowing but doing it anyway" psychology
RESOLUTION x EXPLAIN emotion: 😌Relief
"I'm not telling you to stop staying up late — I'm saying learn to repair the damage after"
← Soul influence: Cost conflict resolution is "reduce the cost" not "stop the behavior"
VALUE x DEMO emotion: ✨Hope
"Apply a layer before bed — the next day, eye area looks so much better..." (show results)
← Soul influence: CONTROL motivation — make users feel they can "control the cost of staying up late"
CTA x URGENCY emotion: 💪Action
"A night owl essential — the sooner you start, the sooner you repair. Link is here!"
← Soul influence: TA identity (night owl) echoed for confidence + reinforcement
M03-7-fullcase3.presenterContent: ▶ Presenter Key Point: The resolution to cost conflict is not "stop doing it" but "you can still fix the damage" — give users a psychological way out.
M03-7-fullcase3.notes: Eye cream case — demonstrates the creative logic of cost conflict type.

M03-7-fullcase4.title: Live Case 4: Foundation | Comparison Conflict
M03-7-fullcase4.studentContent:
🎯 Soul Core (Holistic)
TA: 22-32yo women seeking natural makeup look
Scenario: Doing makeup before going out, wanting a "no-makeup" look
Motivation: beauty (look better without looking fake)
Conflict: Comparison (bare face looks bad vs. makeup looks fake)
Arc: Torn→Curious→Amazed→Captivated
📐 Skeleton: HVC
HOOK → VALUE → CTA (cosmetics: short and sharp)
HOOK x CONTRAST emotion: 🤔Torn→Curious
"Bare face has no glow, but makeup looks too fake?"
← Soul influence: Comparison conflict as the HOOK directly — the dilemma creates resonance
VALUE x DEMO+CONTRAST emotion: 😍Amazed
"Watch — just this one product, skin looks like it has a built-in filter..." (half-face comparison)
← Soul influence: beauty motivation shown through visual comparison — half-face method is the best strategy
VALUE cont. x PROOF emotion: 😍Sustained amazement
"Skin-nourishing ingredients — the more you use it the better your skin gets. Colleagues keep asking if I'm bare-faced"
← Soul influence: TA wants "no-makeup look" — social proof reinforces
CTA x URGENCY emotion: 💓Captivated
"Want that 'naturally perfect skin' effect? Link is right here!"
← Soul influence: beauty motivation's ultimate promise — "naturally perfect skin" not "good makeup"
M03-7-fullcase4.presenterContent: ▶ Presenter Key Point: Comparison conflict is perfect for cosmetics — HOOK directly uses the dilemma, VALUE uses visual comparison (half-face method) to resolve it.
M03-7-fullcase4.notes: Foundation case — demonstrates the creative logic of comparison conflict type.

M03-7-fullcase5.title: Live Case 5: Cleansing Oil | Internal Conflict
M03-7-fullcase5.studentContent:
🎯 Soul Core (Holistic)
TA: 20-30yo self-proclaimed lazy but skin-conscious women
Scenario: Coming home late at night too tired to remove makeup
Motivation: time (effortless yet worry-free)
Conflict: Internal (want to skip it vs. fear of clogged pores)
Arc: Torn→Empathy→Relief→Action
📐 Skeleton: HBVC
HOOK → BRIDGE → VALUE → CTA
HOOK x PAIN emotion: 😩Torn
"Wore makeup all day, just want to collapse at home, but scared of breakouts if I skip removing it..."
← Soul influence: Internal conflict's dilemma — "want vs. fear" psychological tug-of-war
BRIDGE x STORY emotion: 🥹Empathy
"I used to be the same — kept putting off makeup removal, until one time I broke out all over from clogged pores..."
← Soul influence: TA's real state + internal conflict's "consequences" builds empathy
VALUE x DEMO emotion: 😌Relief
"Two pumps, one swipe across the face, it emulsifies instantly — done in 30 seconds!"
← Soul influence: time motivation means emphasizing "quick and effortless" + resolves internal conflict
CTA x URGENCY emotion: 💪Action
"Lazy-girl essential — makeup removal is never a chore again. Link for my fellow lazy queens!"
← Soul influence: TA identity (self-proclaimed lazy) for relatability + "never a chore" echoes time motivation
M03-7-fullcase5.presenterContent: ▶ Presenter Key Point: Internal conflict's HOOK should voice the dilemma out loud. VALUE should solve both sides — both effortless AND no clogged pores.
M03-7-fullcase5.notes: Cleansing oil case — demonstrates the creative logic of internal conflict type.

M03-7-fullcase6.title: Live Case 6: Body Lotion | External Conflict
M03-7-fullcase6.studentContent:
🎯 Soul Core (Holistic)
TA: 20-35yo women with dry skin in fall/winter
Scenario: Wearing dark tights/clothes and noticing flaking
Motivation: confidence (smooth skin, no embarrassment)
Conflict: External (dry season vs. wanting to dress up)
Arc: Embarrassment→Empathy→Reassurance→Action
📐 Skeleton: HBVC
HOOK → BRIDGE → VALUE → CTA
HOOK x PAIN emotion: 😳Embarrassment
"Wore black tights out, looked down — white flakes everywhere..."
← Soul influence: Specific embarrassing scenario + external conflict's "enemy" (dryness)
BRIDGE x STORY emotion: 🥹Empathy
"Every fall and winter it is the same — legs flaking like a snowstorm..."
← Soul influence: TA's seasonal pain point + humorous metaphor builds rapport
VALUE x DEMO+PROOF emotion: 😌Reassurance
"Apply a layer after showering — the next day, legs smooth as a peeled egg"
← Soul influence: confidence motivation → emphasize "smoothness" + vivid metaphor
CTA x URGENCY emotion: 💪Action
"Fall/winter must-have — say goodbye to 'snowstorm legs.' Link is right here!"
← Soul influence: seasonal timing + callbacks to HOOK's pain point word "snowstorm legs" for bookending
M03-7-fullcase6.presenterContent: ▶ Presenter Key Point: External conflict works perfectly for seasonal products — HOOK names the "enemy," VALUE provides the "weapon," CTA emphasizes timing.
M03-7-fullcase6.notes: Body lotion case — demonstrates the creative logic of seasonal external conflict.

M03-7-summary.title: Module Summary
M03-7-summary.studentContent:
Field 1

target_audience

"Who to move?"
One video moves one type of person

Field 2

trigger_scenario

"When does it resonate?"
The more specific the scenario, the deeper the resonance

Field 3

reward_type

"What do they truly want?"
Psychological needs beyond the product

Field 4

conflict_type

"Where is the tension?"
No conflict, no appeal

Field 5

emotion_arc

"How do emotions shift?"
Emotions are designed, not accidental

Bone solves "how to tell," Soul solves "why it works"
Design the Soul first (know who to move), then choose the Bone (decide how to tell it)
M03-7-summary.notes: Summarize the core takeaways of all 5 fields.

M03-end.title: Module 03 Complete
M03-end.subtitle: Soul System
M03-end.description: Next Module: Meat System — How it looks & sounds
M03-end.notes: Module complete — transition to the next module.
